# UC-21.8.20 В· Call Failure Root Cause Correlation (SIP + CDR + Element Logs)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Quality &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When calls start failing in bunches, we line up phone-switch error messages with the red lights from the gear that carries calls between cities. That way fix-it crews know whether the problem is busy lines, a wrong route, or a broken boxвЂ”not guesswork.*

---

## Description

Rolls up failed session-invite outcomes by fifteen-minute buckets and trunk group, joins recent network-management alarms on the same trunks, and separates client-class versus server-class errors so outage triage distinguishes congestion, routing, and endpoint-heavy symptoms.

## Value

Operations restores voice services faster during major incidents by seeing which trunk groups simultaneously show signaling failures and underlying transport or switch faults, cutting mean time to innocence for peers and shortening customer-impact windows.

## Implementation

Guarantee `call_id` and `trunk_group` extract consistently across probes and cores; widen join tolerance with `nearest` buckets if clocks skew; add optional lateral reference to disposition-rich raw records using call identifiers when drills demand deeper proof.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- SIP logs with numeric **status** (4xx/5xx), **`call_id`**, **`trunk_group`**, timestamps NTP-synced.
- NMS alarms with **`alarm_severity`**, **`alarm_text`**, and normalised **`trunk_group`** (or NE name mappable).
- Optional CDR disposition with **cause codes** aligned by time and numbering.
- Heavy forwarders sized for **peak EPS**; consider **EDGE** filtering.
- Roles: ops `indexes=voip` + `nms`; SOC may be read-only aggregated.
- Runbook linking **transport** vs **signalling** vs **subscriber** fault patterns.
- License: voice core syslog can dominate вЂ” **sample** non-INVITE noise at source where safe.

### Step 1 вЂ” Configure data collection
**SBC syslog**

```ini
[udp://:514]
connection_host = dns
index = voip
sourcetype = sip:invite
no_appending_timestamp = true
```

**props.conf**

```ini
[sip:invite]
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
TIME_PREFIX = ^\S+\s+\d+\s+\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
EXTRACT-sip_status = SIP/\d\.\d\s+(?P<status>\d{3})
EXTRACT-call_id = [Cc]all-ID:\s*(?P<call_id>[^\r\n]+)
EXTRACT-trunk = trunk=(?P<trunk_group>[^\s;]+)
```

**NMS traps** via SC4SNMP or UDP:

```ini
[snmptrap://:162]
index = voip
sourcetype = nms:alarm
```

Map `varbind` в†’ `alarm_text`, `severity` в†’ `alarm_severity`, `ne_name` в†’ join key for trunk via lookup.

**Verification**

```spl
index=voip sourcetype=sip:invite earliest=-15m
| stats count dc(call_id) dc(trunk_group)

index=voip sourcetype=nms:alarm earliest=-15m
| stats count by alarm_severity
```

### Step 2 вЂ” Create the search and alert

```spl
index=voip sourcetype=sip:invite status>=400 NOT [
    inputlookup sip_maintenance_windows WHERE now()>=start AND now()<=end
    | fields trunk_group
  ]
| eval failure_class=case(
    status>=400 AND status<500, "client_error",
    status>=500 AND status<600, "server_error",
    1=1, "other")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count AS sip_failures, dc(call_id) AS failed_calls, values(status) AS error_codes
    BY _time, trunk_group, failure_class
| join type=left _time, trunk_group [
    search index=voip sourcetype=nms:alarm alarm_severity IN ("critical","major")
    | eval trunk_group=coalesce(trunk_group, ne_trunk_map)
    | bin _time span=15m
    | stats count AS alarms, values(alarm_text) AS alarm_details BY _time, trunk_group
  ]
| where sip_failures > 50 OR alarms > 0
| sort - sip_failures
```

#### Understanding this SPL
- **Maintenance subsearch** removes expected failures during change windows.
- **`failure_class` case** separates **customer-edge** vs **core** behaviour for runbook routing.
- **`bin 15m`** aligns SIP aggregates to **NMS poll/trap** cadence; tune to your ENM/NetAct granularity.
- **`join`** brings concurrent **infrastructure faults**; if clocks skew, use **`| join max=0 ... [search | eval _time=_time+300]`** experimental tolerance or `nearest` pattern with `streamstats`.
- **`values(status)`** can explode cardinality вЂ” switch to `max(status)` for executive dashboards.
- **Threshold 50** failures/15m/trunk is example вЂ” scale to **baseline * 3**.

Schedule as Alert: cron `*/5 * * * *`, range **`-30m`**, throttle **30m** per `trunk_group`, trigger if **result>0**.

### Step 3 вЂ” Validate
(a) Replay **known incident** PCAP-derived syslog slice вЂ” spikes must align buckets.
(b) Force **lab OPTIONS storm** вЂ” confirm filtered out.
(c) Validate **alarm join** by injecting test trap mapped to trunk.
(d) Compare **CDR disposition** uplift when adding optional subsearch for same `call_id` prefix window.
(e) Cardinality check: `| stats dc(error_codes)` should stay **<20**; else refine extraction.
(f) Role-based view: **SOC** sees masked trunks if needed.

### Step 4 вЂ” Operationalize
Four-panel dashboard per **visualization**. Paging to **voice bridge** with runbook deep links; **Slack** stream for situational awareness.
Runbook: (1) Classify **4xx vs 5xx**. (2) Check **peer trunk health**. (3) If alarms present, open **transmission** ticket. (4) If customer-only, engage **enterprise care**. (5) Escalate **IMS core** if `server_error` cluster-wide. (6) Post-incident **tap** batch review.

Voice core signalling triage extensions and multi-vendor coexistence

SIP ladders across Oracle BC-SBC (Acme Packet heritage), Ribbon SBC SWe, Huawei SBC, and Cisco IOS-XE CUBE behave differently in syslog formattingвЂ”even delimiters vary. Maintain **per-vendor TA** overlays or consolidated `Transforms.conf` keyed by syslog `host`/`program` stamping `vendor_family` extracted at ingestion. Regression-test extractions quarterly using anonymised PCAPв†’text fixtures because vendor micro-patches subtly reorder headers.

Correlation with IMS `cdr:raw` disposition often requires aligning **invite transaction** versus **successful BYE QoS**. False divergences appear when SIP fails early but bearer succeeds on fallback PSTN gatewaysвЂ”operators sometimes prefer customer experience metrics over pure SIP fail counts. Maintain dual thresholds: signalling-only vs hybrid voice-quality composite if ITU-T-aligned measurements exist (MOS proxies not in base UC but optional extension).

SNMP trap bursts from Ericsson ENM or Nokia NetAct during software upgrades may synchronise artificially with benign SIP anomalies when scripted tests exercise trunks concurrently. Robust operational pattern: annotate maintenance windows keyed not only trunk_group but also **EMS cluster** identifiers from trap varbind translations.

Distributed denial experiments on signalling edges may produce client_error storms without infra alarms until transport policing triggersвЂ”coordinate with SOC playbooks distinguishing pen-test sanctioned windows via netflow correlations or SOAR ticketing tags.

Carrier routing policy changes manipulating **ENUM/DNS NAPTR** lookups surface as clustered 404/486 responses geographicallyвЂ”supplement dashboards with traceroute-derived region tags if obtainable.

Capacity planning interplay: prolonged **server_error** skew may indicate pooled CPU starvation on session border VMsвЂ”bridge alert to virtualization capacity UC family after sustained episodes even if SNMP alarms tardy due to SNMP community throttling behind firewallsвЂ”consider parallel streaming telemetry Prometheusв†’HEC overlays.

Operational postmortems should capture **invite-to-post-dial-delay** deltas when availableвЂ”they distinguish DNS lag from trunk congestion better than naive failure counts alone.

Throughput and parsing cost: syslog flood control via **indexed field filtering** upstream (heavy forwarder whitelist `method IN ("INVITE","BYE","CANCEL","UPDATE")`) cuts noise 40вЂ“70% versus full raw mirroring depending on chatter mix.

Executive communications: summarise incident impact as **subscriber-attempt equivalents** multiplying failed_calls Г— estimated average revenue per minute only when ethically approvedвЂ”financial overlay optional but frequently requested by CXO dashboards.

Compliance for lawful intercept environments: NEVER mirror intercept tagged events into shared operational indexes referenced by this UC; enforce separate secure partition with cryptographic isolation.
Voice reliability collaboration and ingestion hardening

VRE versus SOC paging trees avoid duplicate bridges when signalling bursts overlap pen-testsвЂ”tie into SOAR metadata for suppression expansions evaluated at runtime.

Supplement ICMP unreachable correlation when SNMP is coarse-middling middlebox ageing issues hide from trap cadenceвЂ”but keep companion sourcetypes low volume.

Dashboard colour cues: amber for client_error heavy customer-edge suspicion; red for concurrent major infra alarms implying core escalation.

Heavy syslog volume: Edge Processor rewriting vendor noise lowers license burn while archiving raw vendor feeds cold if compliance mandates immutability.

Lookup drift detectors compare EMS inventories to Splunk distinct trunk_group cardinality for early mapping rot.

Regional traffic surges cultural calendar overlays damp alerts unless infra alarms escalate anyway.

Throughput caution: widen fifteen-minute aggregation only after companion real-time micro-alerts defend sub-minute SLA trunks mandated by regulators.

Ethical segregation reiterate lawful intercept indexes never bleed into correlation searchesвЂ”automated nightly role boundary tests advised.
Layered correlation classes and operational escalation hygiene

Populate `correlation_class` (for example signalling_only, signalling_plus_transport, customer_edge_likely, maintenance_expected, capacity_suspect) combining maintenance hits, SNMP presence, ratios of client versus server errors, and optional hypervisor saturation metrics when available. Executive rollups summarise class mix without burying nuanced engineering tables.

Executive trend panels may EWMA-smooth `sip_failures` while alerting still evaluates raw instantaneous countsвЂ”prevent dashboard panic theatre without sacrificing detection.

Extend later with RTP loss or MOS proxies on separate sourcetypes joined lightly by bucket and trunk to catch patterns where signalling looks clean but media dies.

SNMP loss mitigation mirrors EMS syslog alarm streams deduplicated via `alarm_fingerprint`.

SBC licence or CPU starvation often surfaces as clustered 5xxвЂ”join VM utilisation dashboards where accessible and annotate `correlation_class` accordingly.

Executive views cap trunk lists via scored impact approximations using concurrent call estimates from sampled CDRs when privacy rules allow aggregates only.

Pen-test windows integrate `pen_test_active` lookup time windows to mute customer-facing fallout.

DR search heads replay correlation searches quarterly against thawed bucketsвЂ”voice incidents disregard convenient maintenance windows.

Maintain strict separation between investigative indexes and privileged intercept realmsвЂ”automated nightly boundary tests verifying roles cannot traverse partitions accidentally.

Closing operational maxim: correlate symptoms (`sip_failures`, alarms) but ticket root causes only after disciplined evidence spans customer-edge, trunk, routing, signalling, EMS, and change-control layersвЂ”not premature blame.
Appendix вЂ” vendor log samples, PCAP pivot discipline, and SLO bridging

Maintain an internal library of anonymised **`_raw` exemplars** per SBC firmware releaseвЂ”when patching, diff extractions anticipating regex breakage before production promotion.

Lawful PCAP pivot discipline: Splunk stores only **correlation identifiers** referencing external PCAP repositories compliant with lawful retentionвЂ”never bulk packet payloads in searchable indexes referenced by operational correlation unless explicitly sanctioned.

Translate engineering SIP/NMS anomalies into customer-impacting **SLO minutes** optionally by multiplying failed call attempts by empirical **mean service duration** only as directional estimateвЂ”attach confidence disclaimers automatically in ticketing templates bridging network and CX teams.

Rolling **Firmware Risk Register** keyed by trunk_group-associated SBC version highlights elevated alert sensitivity weeks post-upgrade until stabilisation KPIs revert to baseline variance envelopes.

Throughput: offload verbose SIP debug tiers (`sourcetype=sip:debug`) to colder storage searched only on investigative drillвЂ”not the primary correlation alertвЂ”preserving hotspot performance.

Operational partnership with interconnect peers: exchanging **truncated syslog samples** mutually under NDA during joint incidents avoids attribution arguments prolonging outagesвЂ”document such sharing prerequisites in geopolitically sensitive markets carefully.

SNMPv3 credential rotation mirrored with Splunk Modular Input configsвЂ”automate via CI/CD pipeline templates preventing midnight expiry outages invisible until alarm joins fail asymmetrically confusing triage narratives.
SLO translation, evidence retention, and firmware governance

Optionally publish **estimated not-completed call minutes** to executives using conservative multipliers documented as directionalвЂ”not financial commitmentsвЂ”derived from sampled active call stats joined to SIP failures avoiding overclaim precision.

Maintain **immutable evidence index** capturing top correlation rows per major incident ID for seven years aligning with contractual interconnect dispute windowsвЂ”separate from high-volume ephemeral voip hot storage.

Firmware governance: annotate `sbc_firmware` extracted from syslog banner on each trunk_group correlated row; escalate faster when anomalies cluster post-upgrade until burn-in KPIs stabilise.

Add **ICMP correlation** supplementary low-volume sourcetype probing SBC signalling IPs toward upstream routersвЂ”helps detect black-holing mistaken ACL deploys mimicking signalling storms without SNMP visibility.

Conduct six-monthly **regex regression** harness feeding golden `_raw` samples through props logic in CIвЂ”not native Splunk but scripts mirroring extractsвЂ”preventing unnoticed vendor syslog format tweaks.

Operational closure: summarise incidents with chronological timeline overlaying SIP failure buckets, SNMP alarms, and change ticketsвЂ”timeline visual reduces MTTR debates in retrospective reviews.
Benign noise scoring without deleting evidence

KV-store scanner, pen-test, and OPTIONS-probe ranges; compute `noise_score` only in generation searches to tune paging. Keep raw `voip` events unless SOAR explicitly directs edge dropsвЂ”forensic defensibility matters in interconnect disputes.

Refresh ranges monthly; monitor calibration drift as a joint reliability KPI with SOC.

Do not auto-close incidents on low `noise_score` alone when major EMS alarms persistвЂ”pen-tests overlap real impairments.

Publish monthly unfiltered failure totals to capacity leadership even if daily paging uses noise-adjusted subsets.

Evidence packs, PCAP discipline, carrier bridges, retrospectives

Author runbook macros exporting fifteen-minute SIP/NMS buckets to CSV for interconnect bridge callsвЂ”include trunk_group, failure_class, aggregated counts, EMS alarm excerpts, correlated change ticket IDsвЂ”not full payloads unless legally sanctioned.

Lawful PCAP request workflow should generate immutable ticket references logged back into Splunk via HEC enriching correlation events when captures completeвЂ”maintains investigative trace minimising speculative blame during carrier disputes.

Post-incident learning: summarise root cause taxonomy fields (`rc_transport`,`rc_vendor_bug`,`rc_customer`,`rc_security`) aggregated monthly in SplunkвЂ”steers capex toward chronic classes rather than reacting only tactically incident-by-incident.

Noise discipline: annotate auto-closed benign incidents with reasoning codes (`OPTIONS_maintenance`,`scanner_noise`) searchable laterвЂ”prevent silent knowledge loss implying future regressions unnoticed.

Throughput: bifurcate ultra-high-volume trunks onto dedicated ingestion pipelines preserving smaller customer trunks unaffected when backpressure manifestsвЂ”prevent single noisy trunk starving others ingestion fairness engineering virtue.

Operational security: correlate SIP anomalies with IDS signals only on segregated indexes; never blend lawful-intercept metadata with operational correlations. Schedule nightly automated RBAC regression tests verifying roles cannot traverse partition boundaries unintentionally.

Operational training: replay sanitised PCAP-derived syslog into QA monthly to validate extractor upgrades before promotionвЂ”reduces preventable sev1 regressions attributable to regex drift.

Operational compliance and reporting: multilingual executive summaries may reference ITU QoS narratives directionallyвЂ”do **not** equate SPL thresholds with regulatory pass/fail. Engineering alerting limits must be characterised empirically per environment after controlled burn-in, documented with version-controlled search definitions and CFO-quarter baselines archived in Git-backed exports.
Configuration versioning: snapshot `macros.conf`, `savedsearches.conf`, and Alert Manager policies in Git whenever settlement, churn, or voice correlation thresholds changeвЂ”auditors and regression testing both require reproducible baselines tied to CFO sign-off quarters.
### Step 5 вЂ” Troubleshooting
- **Join returns no alarms but obvious transport outage** вЂ” mapping `ne_name`в†’`trunk_group` broken; fix lookup.
- **Massive 4xx from single enterprise** вЂ” expected CPE; rate-limit alert by **source IP** enrichment.
- **status field null** вЂ” regex misaligned to vendor format; test `_raw` samples.
- **Alert storm** вЂ” increase `span` to 30m or require **alarms>0 AND sip_failures>200**.
- **CDR contradicts SIP** вЂ” normal when media fails post-answer; extend correlation window.
- **Trap loss** вЂ” mirror syslog alarm feed as backup input.
- **Clock drift** вЂ” enforce **chrony** on SBC + UF; monitor `_indextime` skew.

## SPL

```spl
index=voip sourcetype=sip:invite status>=400
| eval failure_class=case(status>=400 AND status<500, "client_error", status>=500 AND status<600, "server_error", 1=1, "other")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as sip_failures, dc(call_id) as failed_calls, values(status) as error_codes by _time, trunk_group, failure_class
| join type=left _time, trunk_group [
    search index=voip sourcetype=nms:alarm severity IN ("critical","major")
    | bin _time span=15m
    | stats count as alarms, values(alarm_text) as alarm_details by _time, trunk_group
  ]
| where sip_failures > 50 OR alarms > 0
| sort -sip_failures
```

## Visualization

- **Row 1 вЂ” KPI tiles**: total `sip_failures` last hour, distinct `trunk_group` impacted, max `alarm_severity`, count of **5xx vs 4xx** classes.
- **Row 2 вЂ” Trend**: overlaid timechart of `sip_failures` vs `alarms` per `trunk_group` (15m span).
- **Row 3 вЂ” Detail table**: `_time`, `trunk_group`, `failure_class`, `error_codes`, `alarm_details` with drilldown to raw `call_id` events.
- **Row 4 вЂ” Supplementary**: topology or chord diagram trunkв†”peer AS cluster (if enriched) OR CDR disposition breakdown side panel for corroboration.

## Known False Positives

**(1) SIP OPTIONS/TLS handshake probes** from SBC HA pairs frequently elicit **401/403** that are benign when `Method=OPTIONS`; filter via method, known health-check agents, or change-window lookups. **(2) Enterprise CPE/firewall policing** concentrates **4xx** storms on signalling trunks even when the core is healthy вЂ” fingerprint customer IP prefixes and maintain customer-edge overlays so NOC does not chase phantom core faults. **(3) Internet-wide SIP scanners & pen-tests** inflate failures on publicly exposed gateways; correlate with sanctioned security calendars plus SOC/IP-reputation lookups before escalating voice-core incidents. **(4) Controlled maintenance & migration** moves or route re-homing purposely drop invite transactions; maintenance KV keyed on trunk_group + EMS cluster suppresses paging while preserving forensic visibility. **(5) EMS vs SBC label drift after CMDB renames** causes false-positive joins when alarm text references NE names that no longer match trunk_group tokens spelled in syslog; nightly normalisation lookups plus regex aliasing mitigate. **(6) Clock drift / NTP issues** separate fifteen-minute aggregates so SIP spikes no longer coincide with SNMP traps visually; widen `bin` spans or interpolate nearest-neighbour buckets with documented tolerance macros. **(7) Shared-lab syslog** accidentally lands in production indexes through misrouted IPs; VLAN segregation + `_MetaData:Origin` tagging prevents pollution. **(8) Busy-hour legitimate **487/486** signalling** reflecting user behaviour (Callee busy/reject) can resemble failures if dashboards ignore call scenariosвЂ”join CDR disposition to distinguish intentional user rejection from network impairment. **(9) Cascading failover exercises** purposely stress trunks sequentially; scripted drills should populate known drill IDs searchable to auto-suppress bridging noise. **(10) Media-path-only failures post-answer** may show benign SIP signalling in Splunk yet customer-perceived outagesвЂ”avoid declaring вЂњhealthy coreвЂќ purely from SIP success without optional RTP/MOS overlays when SLA demands it.
